focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 10、INT. O'NEIL'S BEDROOM - CLOSE ON O'NEIL - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: O'Neil, terminally ill, persuades reluctant retired coach McGinty to lead a replacement team during a strike, promising full autonomy and no Notre Dame stories in return.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: reluctant return triggered by personal appeal
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Agrees to come out of retirement to coach a replacement team after being personally asked by dying team owner O'Neil, securing full control over player recruitment and coaching decisions.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To build a team that plays for love of the game, with full autonomy and no interference.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Committed to leading the Redskins' replacement team during the strike, with authority to recruit any players he chooses and without interference from ownership.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Internal conflict between his comfortable retired life and returning to the pressures of professional football.
evidence_quotes: I want you to come back as head coach. I fired that asshole Tilden today.
Okay. Here's the deal: you let me recruit who I want, with no interference?
Deal.

relation_updates: Re-established coaching relationship with O'Neil based on trust and shared history
status_updates: Gains full authority over team recruitment and coaching
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A principled coach who values autonomy and integrity over comfort.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: McGinty welcomes veteran Reese Evans, who seeks one final meaningful play, to the new Washington Redskins, marking his return to the team despite plans for rebuilding.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: establishing team-building authority and setting cultural tone
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty rejects conventional roster strategies, introduces his personally scouted list of unconventional players, and accepts veteran Reese Evans' request to rejoin for one final play.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To assemble a team that prioritizes entertainment value and personal meaning over traditional talent metrics.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Team construction officially begins with McGinty in control, Evans added to the roster, and coaching staff directed to find additional players.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension between building a competitive team and accommodating sentimental returns like Evans'.
evidence_quotes: McGinty interrupts, pointing at the list and scoffing that most were cut because they were terrible.
He declares they will build a new team from these individuals—not just aiming to win, but to create a team that will be fun to watch.
McGinty, understanding the sentiment, nods to Pilachowski, who marks Evans’ name in the center circle.

relation_updates: Establishes mutual respect with Reese Evans based on shared values about football
status_updates: Asserts authority over coaching staff by overriding their suggestions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A coach who values personal connection to the game and unorthodox potential over conventional performance.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane Falco, a retired quarterback haunted by past failures, is persuaded by Coach McGinty to return to football, citing his talent and need to reclaim courage despite risks of injury and failure.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: recruiting a broken quarterback with untapped potential
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Confronts Shane Falco about his fear of failure and lack of courage, persuading him to return to football by appealing to his talent and resilience.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure a talented but damaged quarterback who can thrive in a high-pressure replacement environment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shane Falco agrees to return as quarterback, filling the team's core leadership position with high-upside talent.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Falco's psychological fragility and risk of re-injury remain unaddressed and ongoing concerns.
evidence_quotes: McGINTY I'm running the Redskins again. And I want you to quarterback 'em.
McGINTY Courage. I think that Sugar Bowl took it all out of you.
You're still young. You still got bags of talent. If you do well, who knows what will happen when the strike ends?
Wouldn't you rather get hurt than scrape the shit off of other guys' toys?

relation_updates: Initiates mentor-mentee dynamic with Shane Falco
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A mentor who sees potential in broken players and challenges them to grow.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Franklin drops passes in practice, Lee overpowers a sled, Bateman attacks a player, and Franklin fails to catch a touchdown pass in the end zone.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty tells Shane he will no longer be used as a drop-back passer and instead will operate as a scrambler, similar to his college days, with an emphasis on rollout plays to improve his safety and effectiveness.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reshape offensive strategy around mobile quarterback
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shane accepts McGinty's new approach and expresses interest in the revised role
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the rollout strategy will be effective against elite defenses
evidence_quotes: SHANE I'm very interested in that.

relation_updates: establishes coaching trust with Shane Falco
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A pragmatic innovator who adapts strategy to player strengths.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane struggles with team performance and injury but is encouraged by McGinty, who boosts his confidence; Shane acknowledges McGinty's effectiveness.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty provides targeted encouragement and tactical direction to Shane, countering his frustration and reestablishing trust in the game plan.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: restore Shane's confidence and trust in the game plan
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shane expresses renewed belief in McGinty's coaching with the statement 'You're good. You're really good.'
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: team still trails and must execute under pressure in second half
evidence_quotes: McGINTY (quietly) You got to look for Murray over the middle. You got to trust me on this: he's a big, tough kid with good hands.
McGINTY You still got a whole half left. You're a thoroughbred out there running against a bunch a nags. You can pick these fuckers apart.
SHANE You're good. You're really good.

relation_updates: receives explicit recognition from Shane as 'really good'
status_updates: physically present in locker room, engaged one-on-one
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A calm, focused mentor who restores belief under pressure.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane executes a fake extra point, gets trampled by a defensive end, enabling Murray to score the winning touchdown; Redskins win and celebrate as Shane is helped up, injured but victorious.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: coach demanding final commitment in crisis
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty insists Shane return to the game after injury, using physical and verbal intervention to compel him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: compel Shane to complete the game-winning drive
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shane stands and returns to the field despite injury, visibly unsteady but compliant
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Shane must now execute the final play under physical and mental strain
evidence_quotes: Don't shake your head at me. You are going back in there.
You are gonna run the same god damn play, you are gonna throw the ball to Franklin again and this time, he's gonna score.
You are the only one in this entire stadium who can do it. Do you understand me? You can do something no one else can do.
McGinty grabs some smelling salts from a hovering trainer. He shoves them under Shane's nose.

relation_updates: asserts absolute belief in Shane under extreme duress
status_updates: takes direct physical action to revive and redirect Shane
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A demanding leader who pushes players to transcend their limits

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: McGinty bans striking players, embraces current team; celebration ensues with media arrival and restaurant patrons singing, embarrassing Shane but energizing the team.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: coach making a defining team-building decision
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty announces the permanent exclusion of striking players and affirms the replacement team as the official roster, marking a decisive shift in team identity.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: solidify team identity and loyalty among replacement players
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: replacement players are recognized as the official team, celebrated by staff, media, and public
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: long-term legitimacy of the decision under league scrutiny
evidence_quotes: McGinty taps his glass at the head of the table and stands.
Mister O'Neil and I have agreed to ban all striking players and to continue to go with you guys.
Suddenly, the whole restaurant starts singing.

relation_updates: elevates relationship with Scabskins from transactional to loyal
aligns with O'Neil in leadership decision
status_updates: gains full authority over team composition
shifts team status from interim to official
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A loyal leader who stands by his team against institutional pressure.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: McGinty wants to keep Falco post-strike as Martel's backup, but O'Neil defies union agreements, demanding a win against Dallas despite risks of stadium closure, believing his hungry team can triumph.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: team coach managing player roster and league compliance during labor conflict
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty proposes keeping replacement player Falco as Martel's backup after the strike, signaling his preference for the current team despite union obligations.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: retain Falco on the team post-strike as a backup quarterback
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: plans to keep Falco in a playing role, contingent on overcoming league and union restrictions
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between McGinty's desire to keep replacement players and the requirement to reinstate union players after the strike
evidence_quotes: McGINTY I want to keep Falco after the strike ends. As Martel's back-up.
O'NEIL Let's keep 'em all. The hell with the regulars.
McGINTY You've got to take the union players back once the strike ends. It's part of the collective bargaining agreement.

relation_updates: clashes with O'Neil over union obligations
asserts authority on player decisions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: a pragmatic coach loyal to his current team's performance over adherence to institutional agreements

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The strike is over, and McGinty tells the defeated team they have one half left to play for self-respect before their final game ends.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty announces the end of the strike and frames the final half as a chance to play for self-respect despite the loss.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: motivate team to play with pride despite defeat
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: team faces the last half with no chance of winning, only personal dignity at stake
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the team will respond to McGinty's call for dignity
evidence_quotes: The strike is officially over.
We have one half left to keep from being totally humiliated.
You have nothing but your own self-respect riding on this game.
Here's your swan song, people. How are you gonna sing it?

relation_updates: team - renewed authority
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A leader who redefines success in moral and personal terms.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane expresses guilt over an incident during a football play that injured Roland Lamont, who is taken away by paramedics; McGinty later snubs Shane, showing disapproval.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty silently turns his back on Shane after locking eyes, showing disapproval following Roland Lamont's injury.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: McGinty and Shane are emotionally estranged
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Shane can regain McGinty's respect
evidence_quotes: He locks eyes with Shane for a beat.
And then McGinty turns his back on him and walks away.

relation_updates: Shane Falco - broken trust
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A moral authority who holds players accountable for their actions.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brian celebrates the win in silence, feeling the victory's energy, while Shane shares a quiet, meaningful moment with Annabelle as others smile around them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: McGinty tells Shane to make the final play decision due to the absence of a kicker, explicitly stating that Shane is the leader.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Win the game with no kicker available
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: McGinty affirms Shane's authority by deferring to him on the decisive play
evidence_quotes: McGINTY (V.O.) Someday, you can explain what that was all about. We got no kicker, so you gotta take it in. Your pick. You're the leader.
McGinty is all smiles.
Shane walks up to him and they shake hands.

relation_updates: transitions from authority figure to supporter of Shane's leadership
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A mentor who empowers his protégé to lead independently.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: McGinty, a retired coach, is drawn back into football by the dying owner O'Neil to lead a replacement team during a players' strike. He builds a team of overlooked and unconventional players, instilling a culture of heart, discipline, and fun. Over time, he evolves from a skeptical authority figure into a trusted mentor, particularly for quarterback Shane Falco, ultimately transferring leadership to him. By the end, McGinty stands by the replacement team's identity, defending their legitimacy and guiding them through a final game defined by pride and self-respect.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: O'Neil fires someone in anger, uses oxygen to calm down, and demands to see Jimmy McGinty immediately, with Augustine observing and adjusting his oxygen.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: O'Neil fires Coach Tildon during a heated phone call for showing leniency amid player safety concerns, then demands Jimmy McGinty be brought in immediately.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Replace the current coach with a more aggressive and loyal figure to reassert control before the strike.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: O'Neil reasserts authority by terminating Tildon and initiating a coaching change, while visibly agitated and reliant on oxygen.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Jimmy McGinty will return and comply with O'Neil's expectations.
evidence_quotes: O'NEIL You god damn wimp! You're fired! Get out! Get out! Get out!
O'Neil throws the phone down.
O'Neil takes big gulps of the rich air.
O'NEIL Get me Jimmy McGinty. Get him here. Tonight.

relation_updates: adversarial relationship with Bud Tildon established
reinforced dependent dynamic with Augustine Ripley

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 10、INT. O'NEIL'S BEDROOM - CLOSE ON O'NEIL - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: O'Neil, terminally ill, persuades reluctant retired coach McGinty to lead a replacement team during a strike, promising full autonomy and no Notre Dame stories in return.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: O'Neil secures McGinty's return as head coach by agreeing to full coaching autonomy and promising to stop telling Notre Dame stories.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure a loyal, capable coach to build and lead a replacement team under extreme constraints.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: O'Neil delegates full operational control of the team to McGinty and transitions into a supportive, non-interfering role.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the replacement team can succeed without veteran players.
evidence_quotes: I want you to come back as head coach.
Okay. Here's the deal: you let me recruit who I want, with no interference?
O'NEIL Deal.

relation_updates: re-establishes coaching partnership with Jimmy McGinty
negotiates power-sharing agreement with mutual respect
status_updates: formally commits to using replacement players
delegates full operational control to McGinty

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: McGinty and O'Neil debate team issues, including lacking a safety despite a game in five days; O'Neil claims he contacted the Governor of Maryland, while Shane impressively throws a perfect spiral.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: O'Neil voices concern about Shane Falco's playing style and the lack of a safety, but ultimately accepts McGinty's decision on the quarterback while asserting he has contacted the Governor of Maryland to help resolve personnel issues.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ensure the replacement team has both heart and competence under McGinty's leadership.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Remains involved in team oversight and identifies key gaps, but defers to McGinty on quarterback selection while taking independent action on other roster concerns.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The team still lacks a safety with only five days until the game.
evidence_quotes: O'NEIL Christ, Jimmy, I ask you to build me a team based on balls and the first player I see is Footsteps Falco?!
McGINTY All he needs is a shot of self-confidence. He's our quarterback. That's the way it is.
O'NEIL What about a safety? We got two a days starting tomorrow and a game in five days! And we still don't have a safety?!
O'NEIL I called a friend of mine who just happens to be the Governor of Maryland.

relation_updates: begins forming opinion of Shane Falco as a player
maintains collaborative tension with McGinty
status_updates: uses political connections to address personnel issues

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bateman penalized for face mask; Shane intercepted again, leading to safety's touchdown; Annabelle's efforts fail as fans leave the game.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coach O'Neil, watching the team's on-field chaos, orders the game to be turned off, expressing that the stress is endangering his health.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Disengaged from the game due to emotional and physical distress.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fear that the team's disarray could further harm his health.
evidence_quotes: O'NEIL Turn it off. This is going to kill me.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane executes a fake extra point, gets trampled by a defensive end, enabling Murray to score the winning touchdown; Redskins win and celebrate as Shane is helped up, injured but victorious.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coach O'Neil physically celebrates the winning touchdown by pounding on Augustine, reflecting a shift from earlier tension to shared joy.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Emotionally reconnected with the team through the moment of victory.
evidence_quotes: ON O'NEIL He looks ten years younger as he and Augustine watch.
ON O'NEIL He pounds on Augustine.

relation_updates: shared celebration with Augustine

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: McGinty wants to keep Falco post-strike as Martel's backup, but O'Neil defies union agreements, demanding a win against Dallas despite risks of stadium closure, believing his hungry team can triumph.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: O'Neil refuses to reinstate union players after the strike, plans to keep replacement players including Falco, and demands a win against Dallas despite the risk of the stadium being closed by the league.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Win against Dallas to prove his team's value and defy the NFL establishment
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Asserts control over team decisions despite his illness and league rules, positioning himself in direct opposition to the NFL's authority.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The league may retaliate by closing the stadium due to violation of collective bargaining agreements.
evidence_quotes: O'NEIL Let's keep 'em all. The hell with the regulars.
O'NEIL The hell I do! I'm dying! Let 'em sue me.
McGINTY They'll do worse than that. They'll close down the stadium.
O'NEIL God damn 'em! Then you got to beat Dallas. The whole country will be watching.

relation_updates: defiant toward union players
in conflict with NFL authority
pushing McGinty to win at all costs
status_updates: seriously ill but still exerting control
using personal funds to circumvent league rules
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant owner using personal resources to bypass rules

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dallas dominates first half with scores from kicks, interception, and fumble return, leading 24-0. Shane struggles under pressure, Cochran is knocked out by Butler, and Washington's line falters.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coach O'Neil watches the game from home, visibly concerned as Washington falls behind early due to Dallas's scoring drives and defensive pressure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: See his team perform well and stay competitive against Dallas
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Remains at home, emotionally affected by the team's poor performance but unable to influence the game directly.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: His health keeps him from being physically present or actively coaching during a critical game.
evidence_quotes: AUGUSTINE massages O'Neil's feet as the old man watches the game. He looks worried.

relation_updates: emotionally tied to team's fate
status_updates: physically incapacitated, limited to watching from home

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brian celebrates the win in silence, feeling the victory's energy, while Shane shares a quiet, meaningful moment with Annabelle as others smile around them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: O'Neil is physically supported by Augustine during the game's climax and shares a kiss with her after the winning play, confirming their close bond.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Physically assisted and emotionally open, showing affection publicly after the team's victory.
evidence_quotes: ON O'NEIL He's being helped back to bed by Augustine.
O'Neil kisses Augustine.

relation_updates: confirmed affectionate relationship with Augustine Ripley
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Vulnerable and affectionate in moments of triumph

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Coach O'Neil, terminally ill and physically frail, drives a high-stakes leadership overhaul by firing the existing coach and recruiting Jimmy McGinty to lead a replacement team during a player strike. He oscillates between emotional volatility and strategic delegation, enduring despair as the team falters before ultimately celebrating a hard-won victory. His journey reflects a shift from direct control to symbolic leadership, grounded in trust, defiance, and a deep emotional bond with his aide Augustine.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane, underwater, imagines throwing a football with the bronze trophy, dodging defenders in slow motion before releasing it and celebrating a touchdown in the muddy seabed.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: While scraping algae from a boat underwater, Shane finds a broken bronze football trophy, which triggers a vivid fantasy sequence where he mimics quarterback actions, signaling, dropping back, and throwing the trophy as if it were a game-winning pass.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally reengaged with his past identity as a quarterback
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: struggles with moving on from past failure in football
evidence_quotes: As he works, he happens to look down and notices something on the bottom.
Shane turns "the ball" over in his hands.
He grips the seams like he's done this before.
Then he holds the football out in front of him. Suddenly, he starts barking garbled signals to an imaginary offense.

status_updates: emotionally tied to past football glory

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane Falco, a retired quarterback haunted by past failures, is persuaded by Coach McGinty to return to football, citing his talent and need to reclaim courage despite risks of injury and failure.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Coach McGinty directly challenges Shane Falco to confront his fear of failure and return to football as the Redskins' quarterback, citing his talent and the need to reclaim courage.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: resisting return due to fear of failure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: contemplating a comeback but emotionally hesitant and noncommittal
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: must confront fear of public failure and regain confidence in his ability under pressure
evidence_quotes: SHANE No, man, I've been out way too long.
McGINTY Courage. I think that Sugar Bowl took it all out of you.
SHANE I'm retired from football.
Wouldn't you rather get hurt than scrape the shit off of other guys' toys?

relation_updates: reconnects with mentor figure McGinty
status_updates: faced with opportunity to return to professional football

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Sid prepares equipment as McGinty questions Shane's oversized helmet, meant for a linebacker, not a quarterback, testing his vision which appears impaired.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane wears an oversized linebacker's helmet and fails a basic vision test, revealing his lack of readiness and misunderstanding of his role.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: appears mentally and physically unready
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he can face the game without psychological armor
evidence_quotes: He's a quarterback! How is he supposed to see?
SID He told me he was a linebacker!
SHANE I can see.
McGINTY How many fingers?

relation_updates: McGinty challenges Shane's competence
status_updates: Shane's physical preparedness is called into question
auxiliary.persona_anchor: fear of physical and emotional harm

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Jamal and Andre intervene when Shane's car is overturned, using threats and gunfire to force the perpetrators to restore the car and cease harassment.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane's car is overturned by striking players, but Jamal and Andre intervene with armed threats, establishing Shane as both a target and protected figure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: survive team hostility
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: socially isolated but guarded by Jamal and Andre
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing hostility from striking players
evidence_quotes: Shane pulls up in a battered '89 Honda.
Wilson motions to another big guy. The two of them proceed to roll the Honda over on its roof. It lands with a crunch.
Andre and Jamal get on either side of Shane. They look at each other and then quickly switch sides. ANDRE Let's go to practice, Shane.

relation_updates: Andre and Jamal become Shane's protectors
Eddie Martel and Wilson Jones confirmed as hostile
status_updates: Shane gains visible support from Jamal and Andre
endures public humiliation but avoids physical harm

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane, scared in his helmet, jumps at a cannon blast as the Redskins' game starts; a small, rowdy crowd cheers.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane visibly startles at the cannon blast during kickoff, revealing intense anxiety and emotional fragility as his first game begins.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: overcome fear and perform under pressure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: nervous and overwhelmed
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he can manage anxiety in high-pressure moments
evidence_quotes: Shane is looking out at the field through the blow-up helmet. He looks scared.
Shane jumps.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bateman penalized for face mask; Shane intercepted again, leading to safety's touchdown; Annabelle's efforts fail as fans leave the game.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After being knocked down in a huddle fight, Shane stands up and demands order by instituting a rule that players must raise their hands to speak, asserting authority as quarterback.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: establish control as quarterback
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: temporarily regains command of the huddle despite ongoing disarray
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the team will consistently respect his authority moving forward
evidence_quotes: SHANE God damn it! I am the quarterback! I am the only one supposed to talk in the huddle!
SHANE Huddle up! Everybody leans in. SHANE If you've got something to say, raise your hand! Is that understood?!

relation_updates: begins to assert authority over teammates
status_updates: emerges as a leader in crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane struggles with team performance and injury but is encouraged by McGinty, who boosts his confidence; Shane acknowledges McGinty's effectiveness.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane expresses frustration about team mistakes but regains composure after McGinty's encouragement, showing renewed determination despite physical pain.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: regain control and lead effectively in second half
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: renewed determination despite pain
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: team instability under pressure
evidence_quotes: We can't put three plays together without a penalty, a fumble of a fight.
You still got a whole half left. You're a thoroughbred out there running against a bunch a nags. You can pick these fuckers apart.
Shane smiles through his pain.
You're good. You're really good.

relation_updates: reinforces trust in McGinty

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Annabelle confronts and punches the Woman in Red, sending both women away; she warns Shane he was about to be robbed, but he reveals he was hoping for that outcome as he enters the apartment.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After being interrupted by Annabelle, who stops two women from potentially robbing him, Shane admits he was hoping to be taken advantage of.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: be taken in by the women
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: aware of being protected but resistant to it
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: desire to be exploited as form of escape
evidence_quotes: SHANE (to Annabelle) These are my guests.
ANNABELLE You were about to be rolled.
SHANE (as he enters) That's what I was hoping.

relation_updates: trust in Annabelle despite initial resistance

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane reveals his fear of failure after a bad game eroded his confidence, affecting his belief in winning or deserving love, until Annabelle reassures him they are winning together.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane admits to Annabelle that his poor performance in a past game led him to believe he doesn't deserve to win or be loved, and he struggles with fear of failure; Annabelle counters by affirming that he is winning her.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rebuilding self-worth and connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: more emotionally open and slightly reassured, though still carrying residual self-doubt
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of failure and belief in personal unworthiness persist beneath the surface
evidence_quotes: Anyway, after that, I could never seem to adjust in the pros. I was too scared. I lost my balls.
I lost the edge you need to play this game.
You're wrong. You're winning now. You're winning me.

relation_updates: gains emotional intimacy with Annabelle
status_updates: begins emotional recovery from past trauma

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eddie Martel assaults Shane, forcing him to promise to stop seeing Annabelle, then leaves with the linemen, threatening Shane before the game.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane is physically attacked by Eddie Martel and forced to promise to stop seeing Annabelle under threat of further violence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: forced to choose between relationship and safety
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically injured and coerced into compliance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: must decide whether to obey the threat or continue seeing Annabelle
evidence_quotes: He kicks Shane viciously in the side. Shane screams.
And one other thing: I want you to stay away from Annabelle.
Martel viciously kicks Shane again. Shane screams.
SHANE (barely visible) I won't go out with her anymore.

relation_updates: enters adversarial relationship with Eddie Martel
relationship with Annabelle threatened by external force

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Shane unites the team with a bold plan to make a statement on the first play of the half; revealing his injuries, he asks for trust and support, inspiring determination in his teammates despite their doubts.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Shane acknowledges his earlier mistakes, reveals his physical injuries, and proposes an aggressive first play to unite the team and confront the Cowboys with renewed intensity.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to earn respect and lead the team to victory
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: gains tentative trust and re-engages the team around a shared goal
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: still needs to prove his leadership and commitment on the field
evidence_quotes: SHANE Gentlemen, our problem is the Cowboys aren't afraid of us. But they should be!
Sid helps Shane strip his jersey off. Everyone reacts to the bruises on Shane's side.
SHANE Somebody has to tape me up.

relation_updates: begins rebuilding trust with teammates
fractured dynamic with Pacifico highlighted

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Brian celebrates the win in silence, feeling the victory's energy, while Shane shares a quiet, meaningful moment with Annabelle as others smile around them.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After a penalty wipes out his touchdown, Shane composes himself, checks on an injured teammate, and calls a game-winning play that gives the ball to Brian Murray.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: secure the win despite setback
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Redskins win the game; Shane is recognized as the decisive leader who delivered under pressure
evidence_quotes: ON SHANE He's walking slowly back to the line of scrimmage.
PACIFICO I broke my arm. (big smile) Thank you. You saved my ass.
SHANE (to Murray) Yeah. You want it, Brian. Let's hook up.
They break the huddle with a roar.

relation_updates: affirms trust in Brian Murray
reassures Mickey Lee
strengthens bond with McGinty

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Shane Falco begins as a broken, isolated former quarterback living in physical and emotional squalor, haunted by a career-defining failure. After being recruited by Coach McGinty, he gradually confronts his fear and self-doubt, transitioning from a reluctant replacement player to a resilient team leader. Despite repeated setbacks—public ridicule, physical injury, sabotage, and emotional collapse—Shane evolves through moments of vulnerability, leadership, and defiance. His journey culminates in a hard-won victory where he overcomes both external opposition and internal trauma, reclaiming his identity not just as a player but as a man capable of connection and courage.
